title: Fig and Roasted Chicory Granita with Fennel Cream
tagline: The espresso granita of a Sicily that lost coffee but kept the habit.
scenario: Hemileia vastatrix, the coffee leaf rust, reached Ceylon in 1869 and destroyed the island's plantations within twenty years. That much is our history. The divergence is a single contaminated consignment of seedlings that landed in Rio de Janeiro in 1875. Arabica had no resistance, the Americas had no quarantine, and robusta was still an obscure Congolese species that nobody farmed. By 1890 commercial coffee was gone everywhere it grew. The habit outlived the plant. Europe had leaned on substitutes before: chicory during Napoleon's blockades, roasted fig in Vienna, barley in every poor kitchen. The cafes changed their beans without changing their hours. Sicily, which had already turned iced coffee into granita di caffe, rebuilt the glass from the bottom up. Chicory root supplied the roast and the bitterness. Dried figs, simmered in the brew, gave it body and a dark caramel sweetness no sugar bowl could copy. A cap of fennel cream came from the seed fields around Catania. Sicilians born after 1890 still call the thing caffe, and nobody corrects them.

items.item: roasted chicory root, granules or coarse-ground (sold as chicory 'coffee')
soft dried figs, stemmed and halved

method: Brew the caffe: combine the chicory, figs, lemon zest, and water in a saucepan. Simmer gently for 15 minutes, then rest off the heat for 10. The liquid should be near-black and smell like a roastery with a fruit stall next door.

Strain and sweeten: strain through a fine sieve, pressing hard on the figs to extract their pulp and body; discard the solids. Whisk in the sugar and salt while warm. Taste: it should be bitter first, sweet second, like a corrected espresso.

Freeze and scrape: pour into a wide metal pan and freeze. After the first hour, scrape with a fork every 30 minutes, breaking the ice into dry, glittering flakes. It takes 3-4 hours to become a pan of dark crystals.

Make the fennel cream: toast the fennel seeds in a dry pan for 1 minute until fragrant, then grind fine. Whip the cold cream with the honey and ground fennel to very soft peaks; it should pour slowly, not hold shape.
Serve: rake the granita once more and pack it loosely into chilled glasses. Cap generously with fennel cream and eat with a spoon, ideally at breakfast, ideally with brioche for dunking.

queries: "barley coffee granita" OR "granita d'orzo" OR "fig coffee" granita recipe
roasted chicory root dessert frozen recipe
verification.verdict: No published granita is built on a substitute-coffee brew of chicory and figs. Nearest neighbors: a chicory-blend coffee granita (real coffee in the base), a muscat wine granita served with fresh figs and fennel syrup, fennel-lemon granitas where fennel flavors the ice itself, and a chicory-cocoa granita mentioned as a suggestion in an ingredient guide. None combine a fig-chicory brew with a fennel cream.
